text.title: Spec Validation and CI Integration
text.summary: Spectral rules, response validation, and CI workflow for keeping spec and implementation in sync.

section.title: Spectral Linting

rule.statement: Run spectral lint on every PR that touches openapi.yaml; treat operation-operationId and oas3-schema as errors, not warnings.
rule.rationale: Missing operationId breaks codegen method names; schema errors produce unusable generated types.

example.code: # .spectral.yaml extends: spectral:oas rules: operation-operationId: error operation-description: warn info-contact: warn oas3-schema: error

section.reference: Full Spectral ruleset with governance rules

rule.statement: Run oasdiff breaking against the main branch spec on every PR; block merge on any ERR-level breaking change unless the commit body contains BREAKING-CHANGE: with justification.
rule.rationale: Silent breaking changes cause consumer SDK failures on the next deploy; automated detection prevents surprise.

example.code: # Detect breaking changes vs main branch git show origin/main:openapi.yaml > /tmp/spec-main.yaml oasdiff breaking /tmp/spec-main.yaml openapi.yaml --fail-on ERR

rule.statement: After regenerating server stubs in CI, diff the generated models against the committed server models; fail the build if they diverge.
rule.rationale: Drift between generated and committed code means the spec no longer describes the implementation, invalidating the contract.
